reflection.quote: The world breaks everyone, and afterward many are strong at the broken places.
reflection.reading:
June has this trick where it stretches the day out so long you can't hide in it. The light is still up at 9pm. There's no early dark to crawl into, no excuse to shut the day down. You just have to keep being a person, awake, present, accountable to whatever Wednesday is asking of you.

When I was drinking, long summer days were a problem. So much daylight, so much time to fill, so many hours where I had to be someone before I could finally be no one. Now they're the prize. A Wednesday in June with nothing especially planned — work, a walk, dinner I actually taste, a conversation I'll remember — used to sound like a punishment. It turns out it's most of what a life is made of. Not the highlight reel. The Wednesdays.

Hemingway's line gets quoted to death, but the part people skip is afterward. The breaking is not the point. What you build on top of it is. Strong at the broken places means the repair holds weight now. You can carry a long ordinary day without needing to escape it. That's not a small thing. That's the whole thing. Show up for the Wednesday. The Wednesdays are where the life happens.
reflection.meditation: Sit with one ordinary moment today and notice you're actually inside it.

affirmations: I can be present for a long, ordinary day without needing to escape it.
Today I am building a life out of the small hours, not just the big ones.
I am strong at the places I was once broken, and that strength holds weight now.

gratitude.prayer: Grateful for a Wednesday I get to be awake for. Grateful for long light, for work that asks something of me, for people who know my real name and not the version I used to perform. The fact that today is unremarkable is the gift — I spent years unable to sit inside an ordinary day, and now I can.
gratitude.closing: One more day, eyes open. That's enough.
